page: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is a bare metal server powered by an AMD EPYC 4345P processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, supported by 32 MB of L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 3890 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics and single-threaded compression tasks placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory performance is mixed; while the PassMark memory score ranks in the top tier, large-block uncached memory write bandwidth is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput consistently register in the average range. For machine learning workloads, the system delivers top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models, though performance scales down to average levels for medium-sized models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal server offers a high density of fast local NVMe storage relative to its compute and memory allocation. The combination of high single-core clock speeds and substantial local storage makes it highly suitable for database hosting, static web serving, and single-threaded processing pipelines. However, workloads requiring intensive multi-threaded memory write operations may encounter bottlenecks due to the lower uncached write bandwidth.
description: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is an x86_64 bare metal server equipped with an 8-core AMD EPYC 4345P processor operating at 3.9 GHz, 16 vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 3890 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and high single-threaded compression speeds, alongside a top-tier PassMark memory score. However, large-block uncached memory write bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25%. Multi-core compute, database operations, and web serving throughput remain average. This server is well-suited for database hosting, web serving, and single-threaded processing workloads.
og_description: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is an x86_64 bare metal server featuring an 8-core AMD EPYC 4345P CPU, 64 GB RAM, and 3890 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for database and web workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is a bare metal server with an 8-core AMD EPYC CPU, 64 GB RAM, and 3.89 TB NVMe storage, optimized for database workloads.
tagline: A bare metal server combining high-frequency single-core performance with expansive local NVMe storage for database and web workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 4345P processor with 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs at 3.9 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory paired with 3890 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Average multi-core compute, database operations, and web serving throughput
Weak large-block uncached memory write bandwidth represents a hardware tradeoff
Best suited for database hosting, web serving, and small-scale LLM inference
